_metadata.description: Permitting events for proposed transmission projects, researched and vetted per Ultan framework
_metadata.sources: FERC eLibrary, Federal Register, DOE/BLM press releases, state PUC orders, court records, Utility Dive, E&E News
vetting_notes: Each event individually sourced with source_type classification
Government sources (tier 1) prioritized; news sources (tier 3) flagged
Industry sources (tier 4) flagged; used only when no government/news alternative
ESA and Section 106 details often in FEIS appendices not publicly indexed — flagged as [GAP]

necec.description: CMP filed Presidential Permit application with DOE
Maine PUC approved Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity
Maine LUPC voted 5-2 to approve site law certification
Sierra Club et al. filed federal lawsuit challenging USACE/DOE NEPA reviews
First Circuit affirmed denial of preliminary injunction
necec.agency: U.S. Court of Appeals, First Circuit

necec.description: Maine voters approved Question 1 referendum (59%) blocking NECEC
Construction halted at Governor Mills' request after referendum
Maine Supreme Judicial Court ruled retroactive referendum unconstitutional if vested rights acquired (2022 ME 48)
Maine BEP denied appeals of DEP permit; modified conditions (50,000 acre conservation, decommissioning plan)
Jury ruled 9-0 that NECEC had acquired vested rights through substantial construction
Federal judge ruled in favor of USACE/DOE, declining to revoke federal permits
Environmental groups appealed federal ruling to First Circuit
necec.agency: U.S. Court of Appeals, First Circuit

sunzia-transmission.description: BLM published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS
BLM published Draft EIS for public review
BLM issued Record of Decision for original route
sunzia-transmission.date_note: [ESTIMATE] Exact date not confirmed; pre-dates 2016 ROW grant

sunzia-transmission.description: BLM published NOI for supplemental EIS and RMP amendments for ROW amendment
SunZia received FAST-41 coverage from Permitting Council
BLM published Draft EIS for ROW amendment
USFWS listed lesser prairie-chicken as endangered (Southern DPS) and threatened (Northern DPS)
BLM published Final EIS for ROW amendment
BLM signed Record of Decision / Approved RMP Amendment
sunzia-transmission.source: Federal Register 88 FR 32169; DOI press release

sunzia-transmission.description: Tohono O'odham Nation and San Carlos Apache Tribe allege BLM issued construction LNTPs before completing Section 106 tribal consultation in San Pedro Valley
Tribes filed federal lawsuit alleging NHPA Section 106 violations in SunZia construction through San Pedro Valley
Judge denied tribes' request for preliminary injunction to stop construction
Ninth Circuit reversed dismissal, ruling BLM's 2023 LNTPs were the final agency action; tribes' Section 106 claims revived
sunzia-transmission.agency: U.S. Court of Appeals, Ninth Circuit

grain-belt-express.description: Kansas Corporation Commission issued initial siting permit for 370-mile KS segment
Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission issued CPCN
Missouri PSC voted 3-2 to deny CCN (first denial)
Missouri PSC denied CCN a second time
Missouri Supreme Court unanimously reversed PSC denial; remanded
Missouri PSC granted CCN to Grain Belt Express (under Invenergy)
Kansas Corporation Commission approved siting certificate extension
DOE published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS (DOE/EIS-0554)
Illinois Commerce Commission granted CPCN, completing four-state siting approvals
Missouri PSC approved amended CCN for expanded scope
DOE LPO announced conditional commitment for up to $4.9B loan guarantee
Illinois 5th District Appellate Court reversed ICC approval, ruling GBE failed to prove financing
DOE published Draft EIS for Phase 1
Missouri AG issued Civil Investigative Demand alleging misleading claims
DOE published Final EIS for Phase 1
Illinois Supreme Court reversed appellate court, reinstated ICC approval
Missouri appellate court ruled Chariton County cannot block construction by denying road-crossing permits

transwest-express.description: BLM and WAPA published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS
BLM and WAPA published Draft EIS (~2,000 pages)
BLM and WAPA published Final EIS
Section 106 tribal consultation initiated; 7 tribes responded. Ute Mountain Ute raised concerns about human remains and sacred sites
Biological Assessment completed as FEIS Appendix N; greater sage-grouse analyzed but not ESA-listed
TransWest sued USDA/NRCS over refusal to consent to condemnation within conservation easement
TransWest sued National Park Service in D. Colorado
BLM signed Notice to Proceed to Construction

cardinal-hickory-creek.description: USDA RUS published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS
Wisconsin PSC voted unanimously to grant CPCN
RUS, USFWS, and USACE jointly issued Record of Decision
Conservation groups filed federal lawsuit challenging FEIS/ROD and USFWS refuge crossing permit
Judge Conley ruled NEPA review inadequate; vacated FEIS/ROD; found USFWS refuge right-of-way arbitrary and capricious
cardinal-hickory-creek.source: ELPC press release; RUS project page

cardinal-hickory-creek.description: 7th Circuit reversed district court, ruling USFWS had not made final action on land exchange; effectively cleared project
Judge Conley issued preliminary injunction blocking USFWS land swap for refuge crossing
7th Circuit stayed district court injunction, clearing land exchange
USFWS land exchange completed (36 acres to refuge, 10 acres for line)

boardman-to-hemingway.description: BLM published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS
Idaho Power filed Application for Site Certificate with Oregon EFSC
Stop B2H Coalition filed suit challenging sage grouse analysis and cumulative effects under NEPA
Oregon EFSC approved site certificate after contested case proceedings

champlain-hudson-power-express.description: DOE published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS
NY Article VII settlement reached with parties
NY PSC granted Certificate of Environmental Compatibility and Public Need
Section 7 consultation found CHPE not likely to adversely affect Atlantic sturgeon
DOE issued Record of Decision / Presidential Permit
Center for Biological Diversity filed 60-day ESA notice regarding Atlantic sturgeon critical habitat
NOAA Fisheries issued updated concurrence upholding original findings on Atlantic sturgeon

cross-tie-500kv.description: BLM published Notice of Intent to prepare EIS
DOE Transmission Facilitation Program selected Cross-Tie (~$226M capacity contract)
BLM published Final EIS; corridor reduced in NV for sage-grouse RMP compliance
TransCanyon terminated DOE TFP capacity contract

soo-green-hvdc.description: SOO Green filed FERC complaint alleging PJM interconnection process caused years of delay
SOO Green filed second FERC complaint alleging PJM capacity import rules block market entry
Iowa Utilities Board approved franchise petition including eminent domain for 6 parcels
Final municipal franchise agreement secured (Bellevue, IA — 24th of 24 Iowa municipalities)

riosol-transmission.description: BLM published NOI for SunZia/RioSol corridor EIS
BLM published Draft EIS (shared with SunZia corridor)
BLM published Final EIS for SunZia/RioSol corridor
BLM signed Record of Decision / Approved RMP Amendment (covers RioSol route)

lake-erie-connector.description: DOE issued FONSI and Presidential Permit (PP-412)
Pennsylvania DEP issued Water Obstruction/Encroachment Permit and NPDES Stormwater Permit
NextEra acquired project; filed to amend Presidential Permit (PP-412-1)
DOD no-objection letter and State Dept favorable recommendation for amended permit

southern-spirit.description: Pattern Energy submitted siting certificate petition to Mississippi PSC
Pattern Energy submitted application to Louisiana PSC for route corridor approval
Louisiana Senate passed SB 108 (36-1) denying eminent domain to transmission lines unless majority of power consumed in-state — targeted at Southern Spirit
Governor Landry signed SB 108 into law, barring Southern Spirit from using eminent domain in Louisiana
Louisiana PSC voted 3-2 to certify Southern Spirit project
DOE selected Southern Spirit for $360M TFP capacity contract

southline-transmission.description: BLM/WAPA published NOI to prepare EIS
Draft EIS and Draft RMP Amendment released
Section 7 ESA consultation with USFWS completed
Additional ESA consultation for subsequently listed species concluded
USFWS issued concurrence on 'may affect, not likely to adversely affect' for newly listed species
DOE selected Southline for TFP Round 1 ($477M Phase 1)
DOE selected Southline Phase 2 for TFP Round 2

cimarron-link.description: DOE selected Cimarron Link for $306M TFP capacity contract
cimarron-link.source: DOE TFP selections page; Invenergy press release

cimarron-link.description: Cimarron Link sued Oklahoma landowners who refused to allow surveyors on property
Cimarron Link dropped landowner survey access lawsuits
Invenergy confirmed project slowdown citing 'evolving market conditions and grid plans'

gateway-south.description: BLM published NOI to prepare EIS
BLM issued Record of Decision and Approved Land Use Plan Amendments (WY/CO/UT)
PacifiCorp paid $34M to NFWF for compensatory sage-grouse mitigation

twin-states-clean-energy.description: DOE selected Twin States for TFP capacity contract negotiations
National Grid withdrew from TFP, stating 'project is not viable at this time'
Vermont DPS Commissioner stated DOE's 'extremely accelerated timelines' contributed to failure — developers could not secure energy buyers

north-plains-connector.description: DOE announced conditional GRIP award of up to $700M to Montana Department of Commerce for North Plains Connector (Interregional Innovation category)
north-plains-connector.source: Governor of North Dakota press release

north-plains-connector.description: DOE Grid Deployment Office and Montana DEQ jointly issued Notice of Intent to prepare Environmental Impact Statement (DOE/EIS-0568)
Public scoping meetings held (Colstrip MT, Miles City MT, Bismarck ND); scoping comment period closed Dec 9, 2024
NorthWestern Energy signed MOU for 10% ownership (300 MW)
BHE US Transmission signed MOU for 10% ownership (300 MW of 3,000 MW)
Hitachi Energy and Grid United announced Engineering Services Agreement for HVDC converter station design (electrical ratings, valve hall layouts, control systems, harmonic studies)
DOE issued Amended NOI clarifying decision-making role related to GRIP conditional award
Draft EIS (DOE/EIS-0568) published; public comment period open through February 23, 2026
north-plains-connector.agency: DOE / Montana DEQ / BLM / USFS / ARS
north-plains-connector.date_note: [ESTIMATE] Published January 2026; exact day not confirmed

swip-north.description: BLM issued Record of Decision for Southwest Intertie Project (covers SWIP-North corridor on BLM-managed land)
swip-north.date_note: [ESTIMATE] Original SWIP ROD date not precisely identified; approximate based on BLM documents

swip-north.description: CAISO approved SWIP-North Participating Transmission Owner (PTO) application
DOE selected SWIP-North for Transmission Facilitation Program — up to $331M capacity contract; first project under CITAP framework
BLM signed decision record for Robinson Summit Substation Expansion and Interconnection (SWIP-North Nevada endpoint)
swip-north.date_note: [ESTIMATE] 2024; exact date not confirmed from search results

swip-north.description: Cassia and Twin Falls county corridors approved for SWIP-North
swip-north.date_note: [ESTIMATE] Approximate date; county approvals confirmed but exact dates not sourced

swip-north.description: Jerome County Commission sustained and remanded special-use permit decision to Planning and Zoning Commission — permit still pending
Idaho PUC approved Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN) for SWIP-North

aroostook-renewable-gateway.description: Maine Legislature tasked PUC with soliciting bids for transmission and generation from northern Maine renewable resources
Maine PUC selected LS Power Grid Maine to develop 345 kV transmission line from Aroostook County to ISO-NE grid
Maine Legislature gave bipartisan approval supporting Aroostook Renewable Gateway
Multiple towns along proposed route (Unity, Albion, Palermo, others) enacted moratoria on high-voltage power lines; landowner opposition to eminent domain
Maine PUC terminated LS Power Grid Maine contract — cost escalation and route/landowner disputes; directed new procurement
Maine PUC issued new RFI for transmission utility and called for developer Indications of Interest
DOE awarded Avangrid up to $425M capacity contract under TFP Round 2 for Aroostook Renewable Project
Maine PUC gave Aroostook project priority status; extended state power purchase deadline from Dec 2025 to 2027
Maine restarted push with new RFP for Aroostook County wind and transmission, better coordinated with ISO-NE planning

pecos-west.description: Grid United Texas filed CCN application with PUCT for Pecos West Intertie (Docket 53758)
PUCT rejected CCN application, ruling Texas right-of-first-refusal (ROFR) law prevents issuing permit to independent transmission developer
Grid United withdrew CCN application following PUCT ROFR ruling
Grid United Texas refiled with PUCT seeking public interest determination (not CCN), arguing interregional ERCOT-WECC interconnection falls outside ROFR scope
pecos-west.date_note: [ESTIMATE] Exact refiling date not confirmed; inferred from ongoing docket activity

three-corners-connector.description: Grid United began stakeholder engagement with over 400 participants including ~300 landowners, local/state officials, and community leaders in SE Colorado and OK panhandle
DOE Transmission Siting and Economic Development Grants Program awarded $9.5M to Colorado DOLA for communities impacted by Three Corners Connector
three-corners-connector.date_note: [ESTIMATE] July 2024; announced as part of TSED program

three-corners-connector.description: Grid United secured voluntary land option agreements for 100% of proposed route in Texas and Cimarron Counties, OK; 97% survey permissions and 90% site control overall
Three Corners Connector LLC filed application with Oklahoma Corporation Commission seeking authority to construct 66.24 miles HVDC + 9.04 miles AC in Oklahoma panhandle (Cimarron and Texas Counties)
three-corners-connector.date_note: [ESTIMATE] January 2026; exact filing date not confirmed

wyoming-intertie.description: Grid United announced Wyoming Intertie project; began landowner meetings in Carbon, Albany, and Platte Counties
Landowner meetings held (March 2023) — route refinement and community engagement in Carbon, Albany, Platte Counties
Wyoming Intertie Community Investment Program established through Wyoming Community Foundation — $200,000 in grants awarded to local organizations
